sentences.sentence: On 8 June 2004, more than half the population of the world were treated to a rare astronomical event.
sentences.translation: 2004年6月8日，世界上一半以上的人口有幸目睹了一场罕见的天文现象。

structure.text: more than half the population of the world
were treated to a rare astronomical event

difficult_points: be treated to 在这里表示“有幸享受到或看到”，并非“被治疗”。
population 是集合名词，原文用 were 强调其中的人。

sentences.sentence: For over six hours, the planet Venus steadily inched its way over the surface of the Sun.
sentences.translation: 在六个多小时里，金星缓慢而稳定地从太阳表面移过。

structure.text: over the surface of the Sun

difficult_points: inch one's way 是形象表达，表示一点点缓慢移动。
over the surface 是从观测者视角描述金星掠过太阳圆面。

sentences.sentence: This 'transit' of Venus was the first since 6 December 1882.
sentences.translation: 这是自1882年12月6日以来首次出现金星“凌日”。
difficult_points: transit 在天文学中指行星从太阳圆面前方经过的“凌日”。
the first 后省略 transit，since 引出上一次发生的时间。

sentences.sentence: On that occasion, the American astronomer Professor Simon Newcomb led a party to South Africa to observe the event.
sentences.translation: 那一次，美国天文学家西蒙·纽科姆教授率领一支队伍前往南非观测这一现象。

structure.text: the American astronomer Professor Simon Newcomb
led a party to South Africa

difficult_points: the American astronomer 与 Professor Simon Newcomb 构成同位命名关系。
to observe... 是目的状语，说明前往南非的目的。

sentences.sentence: They were based at a girls' school, where—it is alleged—the combined forces of three schoolmistresses outperformed the professionals with the accuracy of their observations.
sentences.translation: 他们驻扎在一所女子学校；据说，那里的三位女教师通力合作，观测精度甚至胜过了专业人士。

structure.text: were based at a girls' school
where—it is alleged—the combined forces of three schoolmistresses outperformed the professionals with the accuracy of their observations

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句，修饰 a girls' school；破折号中的 it is alleged 是插入语。
outperformed... with the accuracy... 表示三位教师凭观测精度胜过专业人员。

sentences.sentence: For centuries, transits of Venus have drawn explorers and astronomers alike to the four corners of the globe.
sentences.translation: 几个世纪以来，金星凌日一直吸引着探险家和天文学家奔赴世界各地。

structure.text: to the four corners of the globe

difficult_points: 现在完成时表示这种吸引跨越数百年延续至今。
alike 表示探险家和天文学家两类人都包括在内。

expressions.text: the four corners of the globe

sentences.sentence: And you can put it all down to the extraordinary polymath Edmond Halley.
sentences.translation: 而这一切都要归功于非凡的博学家埃德蒙·哈雷。

structure.text: down to the extraordinary polymath Edmond Halley

difficult_points: it all 回指凌日吸引全球探险家与天文学家的历史。
put A down to B 表示把 A 归因于 B。

sentences.sentence: In November 1677, Halley observed a transit of the innermost planet, Mercury, from the desolate island of St Helena in the South Pacific.
sentences.translation: 1677年11月，哈雷在南太平洋荒凉的圣赫勒拿岛观测了最内侧行星水星的凌日。

structure.text: a transit of the innermost planet
from the desolate island of St Helena in the South Pacific

difficult_points: Mercury 是 the innermost planet 的同位语。
from 引出观测地点，而非凌日运动的起点。

sentences.sentence: He realized that, from different latitudes, the passage of the planet across the Sun's disc would appear to differ.
sentences.translation: 他意识到，从不同纬度观察，行星穿过太阳圆面的轨迹看起来会有所不同。

structure.text: that, from different latitudes, the passage of the planet across the Sun's disc would appear to differ

difficult_points: that 引导 realized 的宾语从句，from different latitudes 是从句中的观察角度状语。
appear to differ 表示从观察者看来存在差异。

sentences.sentence: By timing the transit from two widely-separated locations, teams of astronomers could calculate the parallax angle—the apparent difference in position of an astronomical body due to a difference in the observer's position.
sentences.translation: 通过在两个相距很远的地点测定凌日时间，天文学家团队可以算出视差角——即因观察者位置不同而产生的天体表观位置差异。

structure.text: By timing the transit from two widely-separated locations
the apparent difference in position of an astronomical body due to a difference in the observer's position

difficult_points: By timing... 是方式状语，说明计算视差角所采用的方法。
破折号后是 parallax angle 的同位解释，due to... 说明位置差异的成因。

sentences.sentence: Calculating this angle would allow astronomers to measure what was then the ultimate goal: the distance of the Earth from the Sun.
sentences.translation: 计算出这个角度，就能让天文学家测量当时的终极目标：地球与太阳之间的距离。

structure.text: to measure what was then the ultimate goal
the distance of the Earth from the Sun

difficult_points: Calculating... 是动名词短语作主语；allow sb to do 是宾语加宾补结构。
what 引导融合关系从句作 measure 的宾语，冒号后解释 ultimate goal 的内容。

sentences.sentence: This distance is known as the 'astronomical unit' or AU.
sentences.translation: 这一距离被称为“天文单位”，简称 AU。
difficult_points: This distance 指上一句的日地距离。
be known as 表示“被称为”，or AU 给出简称。

sentences.sentence: Halley was aware that the AU was one of the most fundamental of all astronomical measurements.
sentences.translation: 哈雷知道，天文单位是所有天文测量中最基础的测量之一。

structure.text: that the AU was one of the most fundamental of all astronomical measurements

difficult_points: be aware 后接 that 从句，说明哈雷所认识到的事实。
one of the most fundamental 后省略 measurements，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: Johannes Kepler, in the early 17th century, had shown that the distances of the planets from the Sun governed their orbital speeds, which were easily measurable.
sentences.translation: 约翰内斯·开普勒早在17世纪初就已证明，行星与太阳的距离决定其公转速度，而这些速度很容易测量。

structure.text: that the distances of the planets from the Sun governed their orbital speeds

difficult_points: had shown 用过去完成时，表示开普勒的发现早于哈雷的研究。
which 指 orbital speeds，而不是 planets，补充这些速度容易测量。

sentences.sentence: But no-one had found a way to calculate accurate distances to the planets from the Earth.
sentences.translation: 但当时还没有人找到从地球计算各行星准确距离的方法。

structure.text: a way to calculate accurate distances to the planets from the Earth

difficult_points: 过去完成时说明在哈雷提出方法之前，这一问题仍未解决。
to calculate... 后置修饰 a way，to the planets 与 from the Earth 共同界定距离。

sentences.sentence: The goal was to measure the AU; then, knowing the orbital speeds of all the other planets round the Sun, the scale of the Solar System would fall into place.
sentences.translation: 目标是测出天文单位；随后，只要知道其他所有行星绕太阳运行的速度，太阳系的尺度就能确定下来。

structure.text: knowing the orbital speeds of all the other planets round the Sun
the scale of the Solar System would fall into place

difficult_points: 分号连接目标与由此带来的后续结果。
knowing... 是条件性的分词短语，逻辑主语与主句理解中的 astronomers 相关；fall into place 表示尺度体系随之确定。

sentences.sentence: However, Halley realised that Mercury was so far away that its parallax angle would be very difficult to determine.
sentences.translation: 然而，哈雷意识到水星距离太远，以至于其视差角很难确定。

structure.text: that Mercury was so far away that its parallax angle would be very difficult to determine
that its parallax angle would be very difficult to determine

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导宾语从句，第二个 that 与 so 构成结果结构。
to determine 是主动形式表达被动含义，即“视差角很难被确定”。

sentences.sentence: As Venus was closer to the Earth, its parallax angle would be larger, and Halley worked out that by using Venus it would be possible to measure the Sun's distance to 1 part in 500.
sentences.translation: 由于金星离地球更近，它的视差角会更大；哈雷由此算出，利用金星可以把日地距离测量到五百分之一的精度。

structure.text: As Venus was closer to the Earth
its parallax angle would be larger
that by using Venus it would be possible to measure the Sun's distance to 1 part in 500

difficult_points: As 引导原因从句，its 指 Venus。
worked out 后的 that 从句中 it 是形式主语，真正主语是 to measure...；to 1 part in 500 表示精度。

sentences.sentence: But there was a problem: transits of Venus, unlike those of Mercury, are rare, occurring in pairs roughly eight years apart every hundred or so years.
sentences.translation: 但有一个问题：金星凌日与水星凌日不同，十分罕见；它们约每百年成对出现，两次相隔大约八年。

structure.text: occurring in pairs roughly eight years apart every hundred or so years

difficult_points: 冒号后解释 problem；those 替代 transits，避免重复。
occurring... 补充金星凌日的周期规律，in pairs 与 eight years apart 相互关联。

sentences.sentence: Nevertheless, he accurately predicted that Venus would cross the face of the Sun in both 1761 and 1769—though he didn't survive to see either.
sentences.translation: 尽管如此，他准确预言金星将在1761年和1769年两次越过太阳表面——不过他没能活到亲眼见证其中任何一次。

structure.text: that Venus would cross the face of the Sun in both 1761 and 1769
though he didn't survive to see either

difficult_points: would cross 是从过去视角看未来，符合 predicted 的时态。
though 引导让步从句，either 指两次凌日中的任何一次。

sentences.sentence: Inspired by Halley's suggestion of a way to pin down the scale of the Solar System, teams of British and French astronomers set out on expeditions to places as diverse as India and Siberia.
sentences.translation: 受到哈雷所提确定太阳系尺度方法的启发，英国和法国的天文学家团队启程远征印度、西伯利亚等截然不同的地区。

structure.text: Inspired by Halley's suggestion of a way to pin down the scale of the Solar System
teams of British and French astronomers
to places as diverse as India and Siberia

difficult_points: Inspired... 是原因状语，逻辑主语是 astronomer teams。
a way 后的不定式说明方法内容；as diverse as 强调目的地差异很大。

sentences.sentence: But things weren't helped by Britain and France being at war.
sentences.translation: 但英法两国交战，使情况雪上加霜。

structure.text: by Britain and France being at war

difficult_points: things weren't helped 是委婉表达，意为情况因此更糟。
Britain and France 是 being 的逻辑主语，整个动名词结构作 by 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: The person who deserves most sympathy is the French astronomer Guillaume Le Gentil.
sentences.translation: 最值得同情的人是法国天文学家纪尧姆·勒让蒂。

structure.text: the French astronomer Guillaume Le Gentil

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句修饰 The person，并在从句中作主语。
the French astronomer 与人名构成同位命名结构。

sentences.sentence: He was thwarted by the fact that the British were besieging his observation site at Pondicherry in India.
sentences.translation: 英国人当时正在围攻他位于印度本地治里的观测点，这一事实使他的计划受挫。

structure.text: that the British were besieging his observation site at Pondicherry in India

difficult_points: that 从句是 the fact 的同位语，说明具体事实。
were besieging 用过去进行时，强调当时围攻正在持续。

sentences.sentence: Fleeing on a French warship crossing the Indian Ocean, Le Gentil saw a wonderful transit—but the ship's pitching and rolling ruled out any attempt at making accurate observations.
sentences.translation: 勒让蒂乘一艘横渡印度洋的法国军舰逃离时，看到了壮观的凌日；但船只的颠簸摇晃使任何精确观测都成为不可能。

structure.text: Fleeing on a French warship crossing the Indian Ocean
but the ship's pitching and rolling ruled out any attempt at making accurate observations

difficult_points: Fleeing... 的逻辑主语是 Le Gentil；crossing... 修饰 warship。
rule out 表示排除可能性，pitching and rolling 指船体纵摇与横摇。

sentences.sentence: Undaunted, he remained south of the equator, keeping himself busy by studying the islands of Mauritius and Madagascar before setting off to observe the next transit in the Philippines.
sentences.translation: 他并未气馁，继续留在赤道以南，通过研究毛里求斯和马达加斯加群岛让自己保持忙碌，之后才启程前往菲律宾观测下一次凌日。

structure.text: keeping himself busy by studying the islands of Mauritius and Madagascar
before setting off to observe the next transit in the Philippines

difficult_points: Undaunted 是过去分词形容词作状语，描述主语没有被挫折吓退。
keeping... 是伴随状语；by studying 说明保持忙碌的方式，before setting off 交代随后行动。

sentences.sentence: Ironically after travelling nearly 50,000 kilometers, his view was clouded out at the last moment, a very dispiriting experience.
sentences.translation: 讽刺的是，在跋涉近5万公里后，他的视野却在最后一刻被云层遮住，这实在是一次令人无比沮丧的经历。

structure.text: was clouded out at the last moment

difficult_points: after travelling 的隐含逻辑主语应是 Le Gentil，而主句语法主语是 his view，存在松散悬垂但语义清楚。
a very dispiriting experience 是对前面整件事的同位概括。

sentences.sentence: While the early transit timings were as precise as instruments would allow, the measurements were dogged by the 'black drop' effect.
sentences.translation: 尽管早期凌日计时已经达到仪器所允许的最高精度，测量结果仍受到“黑滴效应”的困扰。

structure.text: While the early transit timings were as precise as instruments would allow

difficult_points: While 引导让步从句；as precise as... 是同级比较。
were dogged by 表示问题持续困扰测量，而非狗的字面含义。

sentences.sentence: When Venus begins to cross the Sun's disc, it looks smeared not circular—which makes it difficult to establish timings.
sentences.translation: 当金星开始穿过太阳圆面时，它看起来像被抹开一样而不是圆形，这使得准确确定时刻变得困难。

structure.text: When Venus begins to cross the Sun's disc
which makes it difficult to establish timings

difficult_points: it 指 Venus；smeared 与 circular 是对比的主语补足描述。
which 指前面金星形状模糊这一整件事；make it difficult 中 it 是形式宾语。

sentences.sentence: This is due to diffraction of light.
sentences.translation: 这是由光的衍射造成的。
difficult_points: This 回指上一句所述的黑滴形状模糊现象。
due to 引出物理原因 diffraction of light。

sentences.sentence: The second problem is that Venus exhibits a halo of light when it is seen just outside the Sun's disc.
sentences.translation: 第二个问题是，当金星恰好位于太阳圆面之外时，会显现出一圈光晕。

structure.text: that Venus exhibits a halo of light when it is seen just outside the Sun's disc
when it is seen just outside the Sun's disc

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，说明第二个问题的内容。
when 从句中的 it 指 Venus，is seen 表示从地球观测到的位置。

sentences.sentence: While this showed astronomers that Venus was surrounded by a thick layer of gases refracting sunlight around it, both effects made it impossible to obtain accurate timings.
sentences.translation: 虽然这一现象向天文学家表明金星被一层折射周围阳光的厚重气体包围，但这两种效应都使准确计时变得不可能。

structure.text: While this showed astronomers that Venus was surrounded by a thick layer of gases refracting sunlight around it
that Venus was surrounded by a thick layer of gases refracting sunlight around it
made it impossible to obtain accurate timings

difficult_points: While 引导让步，说明光晕虽带来科学信息，却妨碍计时。
refracting... 修饰 gases；made it impossible 中 it 是形式宾语，真正内容是 to obtain...。

sentences.sentence: But astronomers laboured hard to analyse the results of these expeditions to observe Venus transits.
sentences.translation: 但天文学家仍辛勤努力，分析这些金星凌日观测远征所得的结果。

structure.text: to analyse the results of these expeditions to observe Venus transits

difficult_points: labour hard 强调付出艰苦努力，不是生产劳动。
第一个 to 表目的；expeditions 后的 to observe 修饰远征的目的。

sentences.sentence: Johann Franz Encke, Director of the Berlin Observatory, finally determined a value for the AU based on all these parallax measurements: 153,340,000 km.
sentences.translation: 柏林天文台台长约翰·弗朗茨·恩克最终根据所有这些视差测量，确定了天文单位的数值：153,340,000公里。

structure.text: based on all these parallax measurements

difficult_points: Director... 是人名的同位语；冒号后的数字是 value 的具体内容。
based on... 后置修饰 a value，说明数值的证据来源。

sentences.sentence: Reasonably accurate for the time, that is quite close to today's value of methods in accuracy.
sentences.translation: 就当时而言，这个结果已经相当准确，与今天通过精密方法得到的数值颇为接近。

structure.text: quite close to today's value of methods in accuracy

difficult_points: Reasonably accurate... 是前置评价，that 指上一句算出的 AU 数值。
原文 today's value of methods in accuracy 表达略显省略或异常，核心意思是该数值接近今天更精确的方法所得结果。

sentences.sentence: The AU is a cosmic measuring rod, and the basis of how we scale the Universe today.
sentences.translation: 天文单位是一把宇宙尺度的量尺，也是今天我们衡量宇宙尺度的基础。

structure.text: and the basis of how we scale the Universe today
how we scale the Universe today

difficult_points: a cosmic measuring rod 是比喻，指 AU 作为测量宇宙距离的基准。
how 引导名词性从句作 of 的宾语，表示今天衡量宇宙尺度的方式。

sentences.sentence: The parallax principle can be extended to measure the distances to the stars.
sentences.translation: 视差原理还可以推广用于测量恒星的距离。

structure.text: to measure the distances to the stars

difficult_points: can be extended 是情态动词加被动语态，表示原理的适用范围可以扩大。
to measure... 说明推广这一原理的用途。

sentences.sentence: If we look at a star in January—when Earth is at one point in its orbit—it will seem to be in a different position from where it appears six months later.
sentences.translation: 如果我们在一月观察一颗恒星——此时地球位于轨道上的某一点——它看起来会处在与六个月后所见不同的位置。

structure.text: If we look at a star in January
when Earth is at one point in its orbit
will seem to be in a different position
from where it appears six months later

difficult_points: 破折号中的 when 从句补充一月时地球的轨道位置。
主句 it 指 star；where 引导名词性关系从句，作 from 后的比较对象。

expressions.text: be in a different position from

sentences.sentence: Knowing the width of Earth's orbit, the parallax shift lets astronomers calculate the distance.
sentences.translation: 知道地球轨道的宽度后，天文学家便可利用视差位移计算出距离。

structure.text: Knowing the width of Earth's orbit

difficult_points: Knowing... 语义上的执行者是 astronomers，虽然主句语法主语为 parallax shift。
let sb do 后接不带 to 的不定式 calculate。

sentences.sentence: June 2004's transit of Venus was thus more of an astronomical spectacle than a scientifically important event.
sentences.translation: 因此，2004年6月的金星凌日与其说是一次具有科学重要性的事件，不如说是一场天文奇观。

structure.text: more of an astronomical spectacle than a scientifically important event

difficult_points: thus 总结前文：现代已有更精确的测距方法。
more of A than B 表示“与其说是 B，不如说是 A”。

sentences.sentence: But such transits have paved the way for what might prove to be one of the most vital breakthroughs in the cosmos—detecting Earth-sized planets orbiting other stars.
sentences.translation: 但这类凌日已经为一项或将成为宇宙探索中最重大突破之一的技术铺平了道路——探测围绕其他恒星运行的类地大小行星。

structure.text: what might prove to be one of the most vital breakthroughs in the cosmos
detecting Earth-sized planets orbiting other stars

difficult_points: what 引导融合关系从句作 for 的宾语，might prove to be 表示未来可能被证明是。
破折号后的动名词短语解释突破的具体内容；orbiting... 修饰 planets。
